---
title: "Publications"
description: "Selected publications, preprints, and manuscripts."
---

## Selected publications and preprints

Edit this page by replacing placeholder links with DOI, arXiv, bioRxiv, journal, or PDF links.

::: {.publication}
### Constraint Semantics for Multi-Level Organization
**Sahil Imtiyaz**  
Preprint / manuscript.

A mathematical framework for multi-level organization based on evolving organizational scaffolds, heterogeneous local state spaces, active interfaces, admissible histories, and local-to-global realizability.

[PDF](#) · [bioRxiv](#) · [BibTeX](#)
:::

::: {.publication}
### The Evolving Reconfigurable Network Bundle: A Geometric Framework for Evolving Communication Systems
**Sahil Imtiyaz**  
Manuscript / preprint.

A geometric framework for evolving communication systems using space-time network complexes, local configuration spaces, boundary-conditioned feasibility, and interface-loop diagnostics.

[PDF](#) · [Preprint](#) · [BibTeX](#)
:::

::: {.publication}
### When is Cross-Scale Molecular Communication Realizable? The Hodgkin Spike as a Cross-Level Signalling Loop
**Sahil Imtiyaz** and collaborators  
Manuscript.

A formal study of cross-scale molecular communication, active biological interfaces, and end-to-end realizability, with a Hodgkin-spike witness.

[PDF](#) · [Preprint](#) · [BibTeX](#)
:::

::: {.publication}
### The Geometry of Viral Release: A Mathematical Framework for Structural Realizability of Virus-Membrane Remodeling
**Sahil Imtiyaz** and collaborators  
Manuscript.

A structural approach to virus-membrane remodeling, emphasizing heterogeneous local state spaces, admissible histories, active membrane interfaces, and endpoint constraints.

[PDF](#) · [Preprint](#) · [BibTeX](#)
:::

::: {.publication}
### The Gauge-Theoretic Structure of Non-Locality and Contextuality
**Sahil Imtiyaz**  
Manuscript.

A bundle- and gauge-theoretic approach to contextuality and local-global obstruction phenomena in physical systems.

[PDF](#) · [Preprint](#) · [BibTeX](#)
:::

## How to maintain this page

For each publication, keep the following information:

- title;
- authors;
- publication venue or preprint server;
- year;
- short 3--5 line description;
- DOI, PDF, arXiv/bioRxiv, code, and BibTeX links.
